test_cases.description: Verifies that a POST /v1/events request with a valid bearer token, Content-Type: application/json, and a non-empty body returns 201 Created with an empty response body and no Content-Type header, and stores the event in SQLite.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the events table
No pre-existing rows in the events table
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer test-secret','Content-Type':'application/json'}, body='{"type":"audit","action":"login"}')
assert resp.status_code == 201
assert resp.body == '' or resp.body is None
assert 'Content-Type' not in resp.headers
row = db.query_one('SELECT payload FROM events')
assert row.payload == '{"type":"audit","action":"login"}'

test_cases.description: Verifies that the service stores the raw JSON body exactly as received without performing any schema validation, accepting arbitrary JSON structures.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the events table
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
body = '{"unexpected_field":true,"nested":{"a":1},"arr":[1,2,3]}'
resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer test-secret','Content-Type':'application/json'}, body=body)
assert resp.status_code == 201
row = db.query_one('SELECT payload FROM events ORDER BY received_at DESC LIMIT 1')
assert row.payload == body

test_cases.description: Verifies that a UUID is generated for the id column and received_at is set to the current UTC time on every insert into the events table.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the events table
System clock is accessible and UTC time is known before the request

expected.db_received_at: current UTC time (within a few seconds of request)

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
before = utc_now()
resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer test-secret','Content-Type':'application/json'}, body='{"event":"test"}')
after = utc_now()
assert resp.status_code == 201
row = db.query_one('SELECT id, received_at FROM events ORDER BY received_at DESC LIMIT 1')
assert is_valid_uuid(row.id)
assert before <= parse_datetime(row.received_at) <= after

test_cases.description: Verifies that the service extracts the bearer token using exactly the 'Bearer <token>' format (capital B, single space) and compares it against AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer my-secret-token','Content-Type':'application/json'}, body='{"e":1}')
assert resp.status_code == 201  # token extracted and matched correctly

test_cases.description: Verifies that when the extracted token matches AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN, the request proceeds to payload validation and storage.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the events table
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer valid-token','Content-Type':'application/json'}, body='{"key":"value"}')
assert resp.status_code == 201
count = db.query_scalar('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events')
assert count == 1

test_cases.description: Verifies that GET /healthz returns 200 OK when the service process is running.
Verifies that GET /readyz returns 200 OK when the SELECT 1 query against the database succeeds.
preconditions: SQLite database is available and connected

test_cases.description: Verifies that PORT, DB_PATH, AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN, and LOG_LEVEL are read from environment variables at startup, with correct defaults applied when optional variables are absent.
preconditions: Service started with only AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN set; PORT, DB_PATH, and LOG_LEVEL unset
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
start_service(env={'AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN':'env-test-token'})
resp = GET('http://localhost:8080/healthz')
assert resp.status_code == 200  # default port used
assert service_config.db_path == './data/events.db'  # default DB_PATH
assert service_config.log_level == 'info'  # default LOG_LEVEL

test_cases.description: Verifies that the service fails to start and emits a descriptive error message when AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN is absent from the environment.
preconditions: AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN is not set in the environment
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = start_service(env={})
assert result.exit_code != 0
assert 'AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN' in result.stderr or 'AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN' in result.stdout
assert no_port_binding_occurred()

test_cases.description: Verifies that the service fails to start with a descriptive error message when LOG_LEVEL is set to an invalid value.
preconditions: LOG_LEVEL is set to an invalid value
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = start_service(env={'AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN':'test-secret','LOG_LEVEL':'verbose'})
assert result.exit_code != 0
assert 'LOG_LEVEL' in result.stderr or 'invalid' in result.stderr.lower()

test_cases.description: Verifies that all log entries emitted by the service are formatted as valid JSON objects.
preconditions: Log output is captured to a buffer or file

expected.log_format: each log line is a valid JSON object

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with capture_log_output() as logs:
    GET('/healthz')
for line in logs.lines:
    assert is_valid_json(line)
    assert isinstance(parse_json(line), dict)

test_cases.description: Verifies that the service honours the LOG_LEVEL environment variable to filter log output at runtime, suppressing messages below the configured level.
preconditions: Service is running with AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN=test-secret and LOG_LEVEL=warn

expected.log_output: no debug or info level log entries appear in output

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with capture_log_output() as logs:
    GET('/healthz')
for line in logs.lines:
    entry = parse_json(line)
    assert entry.get('level') not in ['debug', 'info']

test_cases.description: Verifies that on receiving SIGTERM or SIGINT, the service stops accepting new connections and allows in-flight requests to complete before exiting within the 30-second timeout.
preconditions: An in-flight POST /v1/events request is active when the signal is sent

expected.process_exit: clean exit after in-flight request completes

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
start_slow_request_in_background(delay_ms=500)
send_signal(process, SIGTERM)
new_conn_result = try_new_connection('/healthz')
assert new_conn_result.refused or new_conn_result.error
slow_resp = await_background_request()
assert slow_resp.status_code == 201
assert process_exited_cleanly()

test_cases.description: Verifies that the service force-exits after 30 seconds if in-flight requests have not completed during graceful shutdown.
preconditions: An artificially slow in-flight request is active (takes > 30 seconds)

expected.process_exit: within ~30 seconds of signal, regardless of pending request

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
start_blocking_request_in_background(duration_s=60)
t0 = now()
send_signal(process, SIGTERM)
wait_for_process_exit(process, timeout_s=35)
t1 = now()
assert (t1 - t0) <= 32  # exited within ~30 seconds with small tolerance

test_cases.description: Verifies that the integration test suite includes a test for successful event ingestion that asserts 201 Created and verifies the event is persisted in SQLite.
preconditions: Integration test suite is present in the repository
Service can be started with a test SQLite database

assertions: event row present in events table

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = run_test_suite(filter='ingestion')
assert result.passed
assert '201' in result.assertions_verified
assert 'events table row' in result.assertions_verified

test_cases.description: Verifies that the integration test suite includes tests for missing and invalid bearer tokens that assert 401 Unauthorized responses.
preconditions: Integration test suite is present in the repository
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = run_test_suite(filter='auth')
assert result.passed
assert result.covers_scenario('missing_token')
assert result.covers_scenario('invalid_token')
assert result.http_status_verified(401)

test_cases.description: Verifies that the integration test suite includes tests for missing Content-Type and empty body that assert 400 Bad Request responses.
preconditions: Integration test suite is present in the repository
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = run_test_suite(filter='payload_validation')
assert result.passed
assert result.covers_scenario('missing_content_type')
assert result.covers_scenario('empty_body')
assert result.http_status_verified(400)

test_cases.description: Verifies that the integration test suite includes tests for GET /healthz and GET /readyz under normal conditions that assert 200 OK responses.
preconditions: Integration test suite is present in the repository
Database is available during test run
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = run_test_suite(filter='health_checks')
assert result.passed
assert result.covers_endpoint('GET /healthz')
assert result.covers_endpoint('GET /readyz')
assert result.http_status_verified(200)

property_tests.description: For any POST /v1/events request that passes authentication and payload validation, exactly one row is inserted with the raw payload stored and received_at set to UTC time.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary non-empty JSON strings as the body; use a valid bearer token and Content-Type: application/json for every request
property_tests.invariant_check: after each successful POST (201 response), query events table; assert exactly one new row was inserted; assert row.payload equals the exact body sent; assert row.id is a valid UUID; assert row.received_at is within a few seconds of the request time in UTC

property_tests.description: For any POST /v1/events request with a missing, malformed, or incorrect Authorization header, no row is inserted and the response is always 401 Unauthorized.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate variants of Authorization header values: absent, wrong prefix casing, extra whitespace, completely wrong token, empty string, and non-Bearer schemes
property_tests.invariant_check: for each generated invalid auth header, assert response status is 401; assert events table row count did not increase from pre-request count

property_tests.description: For any POST /v1/events request with a valid bearer token but invalid payload (missing Content-Type or empty body), no row is inserted and the response is 400 Bad Request.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate requests with valid bearer token but: (a) absent Content-Type header, (b) non-JSON Content-Type values, (c) empty body string, (d) combinations of both invalid Content-Type and empty body
property_tests.invariant_check: for each generated invalid payload request, assert response status is 400; assert events table row count did not increase from pre-request count

property_tests.description: For any GET /readyz request, the response is 200 OK if and only if the SELECT 1 query succeeds; otherwise 503 Service Unavailable.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Alternate between states: (a) database available and responding normally, (b) database unavailable (connection closed or file removed); send GET /readyz in each state
property_tests.invariant_check: when SELECT 1 succeeds: assert response status == 200; when SELECT 1 fails: assert response status == 503 and body matches {"message": "service unavailable"}

property_tests.description: For any service startup attempt without AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN set, the process exits with a non-zero status and emits a descriptive error before binding to any port.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Attempt to start the service with AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN unset; vary other env variables (PORT, DB_PATH, LOG_LEVEL) across valid values
property_tests.invariant_check: assert process exit code != 0; assert no socket is bound on the configured port; assert stderr or structured log output contains a descriptive message referencing the missing AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that POST /v1/events with a missing Content-Type header returns 400 Bad Request with an Echo Framework error body and does not write to the events table.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer test-secret'}, body='{"event":"test"}')
assert resp.status_code == 400
assert resp.headers['Content-Type'] == 'application/json'
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert 'message' in body
assert db.query_scalar('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events') == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that POST /v1/events with Content-Type set to a non-JSON value returns 400 Bad Request and does not write to the events table.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer test-secret','Content-Type':'text/plain'}, body='{"event":"test"}')
assert resp.status_code == 400
assert 'application/json' in resp.headers['Content-Type']
assert 'message' in parse_json(resp.body)
assert db.query_scalar('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events') == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that POST /v1/events with an empty request body returns 400 Bad Request and does not write to the events table.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer test-secret','Content-Type':'application/json'}, body='')
assert resp.status_code == 400
assert 'application/json' in resp.headers['Content-Type']
assert 'message' in parse_json(resp.body)
assert db.query_scalar('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events') == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when a database error occurs during event storage, the service returns 500 Internal Server Error with an Echo Framework error body.
preconditions: Database is configured to fail on insert (e.g. read-only file, closed connection, or injected fault)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
simulate_db_failure()  # e.g. chmod 000 on db file or close db handle
resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer test-secret','Content-Type':'application/json'}, body='{"event":"test"}')
assert resp.status_code == 500
assert 'application/json' in resp.headers['Content-Type']
assert 'message' in parse_json(resp.body)

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that POST /v1/events with a missing Authorization header returns 401 Unauthorized and does not write to the events table.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body='{"event":"test"}')
assert resp.status_code == 401
assert 'application/json' in resp.headers['Content-Type']
assert 'message' in parse_json(resp.body)
assert db.query_scalar('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events') == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a malformed Authorization header (wrong casing 'bearer', extra whitespace, or missing 'Bearer ' prefix) returns 401 Unauthorized and does not write to the events table.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
malformed_headers = [
  'bearer test-secret',
  'Bearer  test-secret',
  'test-secret',
  'BEARER test-secret'
]
for auth_value in malformed_headers:
  resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Authorization': auth_value, 'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body='{"e":1}')
  assert resp.status_code == 401
  assert 'message' in parse_json(resp.body)
assert db.query_scalar('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events') == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a well-formed Authorization header with an incorrect token value returns 401 Unauthorized and does not write to the events table.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/v1/events', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer wrong-token','Content-Type':'application/json'}, body='{"event":"test"}')
assert resp.status_code == 401
assert 'application/json' in resp.headers['Content-Type']
assert 'message' in parse_json(resp.body)
assert db.query_scalar('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events') == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that GET /readyz returns 503 Service Unavailable with body {"message": "service unavailable"} when the database connection is unavailable or the SELECT 1 query fails.
preconditions: Database connection is forced into an unavailable state (e.g. file removed or connection closed)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
simulate_db_unavailable()  # remove db file or close connection
resp = GET('/readyz')
assert resp.status_code == 503
assert 'application/json' in resp.headers['Content-Type']
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body['message'] == 'service unavailable'

smoke_tests.description: Full end-to-end smoke test of successful audit event ingestion: a valid POST /v1/events request flows through auth, validation, SQLite persistence, and returns 201 Created.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send POST /v1/events with Authorization: Bearer test-secret, Content-Type: application/json, and body {"type":"smoke","source":"test"}
expected_effects: HTTP 201 Created response with empty body
No Content-Type header on the response
One new row in the events table with payload={"type":"smoke","source":"test"}, a valid UUID id, and received_at set to current UTC time

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for rejection of a POST /v1/events request with a missing Authorization header, verifying the auth middleware returns 401 and nothing is written to the database.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send POST /v1/events with Content-Type: application/json and body {"type":"smoke"} but no Authorization header
mockable: SQLite database (to verify no writes occur)
expected_effects: Response body is {"message": "<error description>"} with Content-Type: application/json
No rows inserted into the events table

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for rejection of a POST /v1/events request with a valid token but missing Content-Type header, verifying 400 Bad Request is returned and nothing is written to the database.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send POST /v1/events with Authorization: Bearer test-secret and body {"type":"smoke"} but no Content-Type header
mockable: SQLite database (to verify no writes occur)
expected_effects: Response body is {"message": "<error description>"} with Content-Type: application/json
No rows inserted into the events table

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the liveness probe: GET /healthz returns 200 OK immediately without database interaction.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send GET /healthz to the running service
mockable: SQLite database (not involved in liveness check)

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the readiness probe when the database is available: GET /readyz executes SELECT 1 successfully and returns 200 OK.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send GET /readyz to the running service with SQLite database available
expected_effects: SELECT 1 query executed against the database

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the readiness probe when the database is unavailable: GET /readyz returns 503 Service Unavailable with the expected error body.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send GET /readyz to the service after forcing the SQLite database into an unavailable state
expected_effects: Response body is {"message": "service unavailable"} with Content-Type: application/json

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for graceful shutdown: sending SIGTERM causes the service to stop accepting new connections, complete in-flight requests, and exit within 30 seconds.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send SIGTERM to the basic_svc process while a slow in-flight POST /v1/events request is active
expected_effects: Service stops accepting new connections after SIGTERM
In-flight request completes and returns a response
